title: verbascum hema-figranum
intro: Verbascum hema-figranum, also known as Indian mulberry or mulberry mullein, is a plant species native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia, characterized by its large, white flowers and thick, velvety leaves. Traditionally, the leaves and roots of Verbascum hema-figranum have been used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, bronchitis, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, Verbascum hema-figranum is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
